Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Penny Heyns backs Tatjana Smith to emulate her historic double

Tatjana Smith can match Penny Heyns’ Atlanta heroics and become just the second person to win both the 100 and 200 metre breastroke at the same games.

Double Olympic gold medallist Penny Heyns believes that Tatjana Smith can match her Atlanta heroics and become just the second person to win both the 100 and 200 metre breastroke at the same games.

Heyns is the only swimmer who has achieved this unique breaststroke double but she feels that Smith already has her matched as she currently reigns aschampionover both distances.

Smith favours the 200 metre and after winning gold over the shorter distance is the favourite to bring home another first-place finish.

“I said she can do the double, but afterwards I looked at the stats. People talk about the double, and I did it in the same Olympics, but Tatjana has now done it, she holds both titles,” Heyns said on SuperSport.

“All I can say is that Tatjana is now the other swimmer who has won 100 and 200 gold [in the breaststroke] at the Olympics. Another South African, which is pretty cool. I know the Americans thought they would be the ones to do it, but I’m just so proud of Tatjana for doing it.”

Heyns lauded Smith’s focus and attention to detail in the 100 metre final when she was clinical with her wall touch helping her to the gold medal by inches.

“We had been discussing the semi-final and how she jammed the touch. But it was the total opposite in the final … she won because she had the most perfect finish,” Heyns said.

“This is a great lesson about how it’s possible to learn from your earlier races.

“You coulnd’t have a more perfect finish, I think the Chinese girl jammed it at the end, but that comes with experience.

“With Tatjana, this is the first Olympics swimming in front of the crowd after Tokyo. It was just a precision race.”

Heyns believes that Smith’s experience will tell in the 200 metre final just as much as over the shorter distance.

“I think she was in the zone, and that is when she is at her best.

“She came out with a smile on her face and I think that was important,” said Heyns.

“Every person is different, so it’s hard for me to say how it might be for her after winning the 100m event.

“But at this level you get used to it. Tatjana has been part of so many international competitions and she has actually won on that stage.

“She has done the race and it’s the past now. She will get home and celebrate and then it’s back to the business at hand. That is what sport is about.”

title: Tatjana Smith to emulate Penny Heyns' historic double
author: James Richardson
description: Tatjana Smith can match Penny Heyns' Atlanta heroics and become just the second person to win both the 100 and 200 metre breastroke at the same games.
